<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String: ; ------------------------------------------------------------
; INTERPRET ( -- ) : Interpret one line of input
; Reads tokens with WORD, dispatches via FIND/NUMBER
; Uses a threaded-code approach for cleaner flow
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84
do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r2, interpret_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; INTERPRET is implemented as a colon word via a thread
interpret_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; returns to caller

; The main dispatch logic — must be a primitive since it branches
do_interpret_dispatch:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [IP]

    ; Check if word is empty (length = 0)
    pop r0               ; c-addr from WORD
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_word
    ; Empty word = end of input, just return
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_word:
    ; Save c-addr for potential NUMBER fallback
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]

    ; Try FIND
    ; FIND expects c-addr on stack, returns (c-addr 0) or (cfa flag)
    ; We need to call FIND as a primitive
    push r0              ; duplicate c-addr for FIND. DS: [c-addr, c-addr]

    ; Save IP on RS, call do_find via mini-thread
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find
    .word do_interp_after_find
    ; (control does not return here linearly)

do_interp_after_find:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save continuation IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; Stack: either (c-addr 0) not found, or (cfa flag) found
    ; Also have original c-addr underneath: (orig_c-addr result flag) or (orig_c-addr cfa flag)
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found

    ; === Found: flag in r0 (1=immediate, -1=normal), cfa on stack ===
    ; DS: [cfa, orig_c-addr]
    ; Drop orig_c-addr: swap, drop
    pop r2               ; r2 = cfa
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [cfa]

    ; Save flag (r0 was popped as flag earlier, but we consumed it in ceq)
    ; Actually after pop r0 (flag) and ceq r0,z, r0 still = flag value
    ; Let's check STATE
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save flag. RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)        ; r0 = STATE
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec      ; STATE=0 → interpreting → execute

    ; Compiling: check if IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2           ; C = (flag == 1 = IMMEDIATE)
    brt interp_exec_compile ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word in compile mode: COMMA the CFA
    ; DS: [cfa], compile it
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save r2 (junk, but need for later)
    pop r0               ; cfa
    ; Store at HERE
    push r0              ; re-push for comma logic
    la r2, interp_comma_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_thread:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_continue

interp_exec_compile:
    ; Execute an IMMEDIATE word while compiling
    ; fall through to interp_exec

interp_exec:
    ; Execute word: cfa on stack
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag (if still there from compiling path)
                        ; For interpret path, this pops cont_IP... that's wrong
    ; Let me rethink the RS management here

    ; RS state depends on path:
    ; From interpret (STATE=0): RS = [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ;   - we need to pop flag, then execute, then return via cont_IP/orig_IP
    ; From IMMEDIATE in compile: RS = [cont_IP, orig_IP] (flag already popped)

    ; Simplify: in both cases, clean RS to [cont_IP, orig_IP], execute cfa
    ; For STATE=0 path, flag is at RS top: pop it
    ; Actually let me restructure. The STATE=0 path jumps here with
    ; RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]. Pop flag:
    ; But the IMMEDIATE path already popped flag and jumps here too.
    ; I need separate labels.

    ; Let me just use a unified approach:
    ; interp_exec is ONLY reached from STATE=0 path with RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag (discard)
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

interp_do_exec:
    ; DS: [cfa], RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; After EXECUTE, we need to continue the interpret loop
    ; Set IP to cont thread that loops back
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute it — when it does NEXT, IP = interp_loop_thread

interp_loop_thread:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; will pop cont_IP, then orig_IP restores caller

interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr_from_find, orig_c-addr]
    ; c-addr_from_find = orig_c-addr (FIND returns it when not found)
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr from FIND
    ; DS: [orig_c-addr]

    ; Try NUMBER
    ; NUMBER expects c-addr on stack
    pop r0               ; orig_c-addr
    push r0              ; keep for error message
    push r0              ; for NUMBER
    la r2, interp_number_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_thread:
    .word do_number
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; DS: [flag, n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag (0=ok)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_got_number

    ; NUMBER failed: print "? " error
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    ; Print "? "
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err_tx1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err_tx2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE to 0
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue interpret loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_got_number:
    ; DS: [n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r2               ; r2 = n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_interp

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    ; Push n, then compile LIT then n via comma
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    ; First push do_lit address, comma it, then push n, comma it
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n again
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile LIT address
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Retrieve saved n from RS
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n was saved 1 slot below current
    ; Hmm, RS is getting complicated. Let me simplify.
    ; After do_comma returns via NEXT to do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]  — n was saved before entering compile_lit_thread
    ; Wait, I saved n but then set r2 to the thread and did NEXT.
    ; The NEXT from do_comma will go to do_interp_compile_n.
    ; When do_interp_compile_n runs, we sw r2,0(r1) saving that IP.
    ; RS: [this_IP, n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; n is at offset 3
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n
    push r0              ; push n for comma
    la r2, interp_comma_cont_thread
    add r1, 3           ; pop this_IP. RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop num_IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_cont_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile n
    .word do_interp_continue_loop

do_interp_continue_loop:
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; Continue interpret loop
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_interp:
    ; Interpreting: number already on stack, continue loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_continue:
    ; After compiling a word, continue the loop
    ; RS: [junk_r2, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop junk
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)</tool_use_error>

    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

file.content:
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; INTERPRET ( -- ) : Interpret one line of input
; Reads tokens with WORD, dispatches via FIND/NUMBER
; Uses a threaded-code approach for cleaner flow
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84
do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r2, interpret_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; INTERPRET is implemented as a colon word via a thread
interpret_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; returns to caller

; The main dispatch logic — must be a primitive since it branches
do_interpret_dispatch:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [IP]

    ; Check if word is empty (length = 0)
    pop r0               ; c-addr from WORD
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_word
    ; Empty word = end of input, just return
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_word:
    ; Save c-addr for potential NUMBER fallback
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]

    ; Try FIND
    ; FIND expects c-addr on stack, returns (c-addr 0) or (cfa flag)
    ; We need to call FIND as a primitive
    push r0              ; duplicate c-addr for FIND. DS: [c-addr, c-addr]

    ; Save IP on RS, call do_find via mini-thread
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find
    .word do_interp_after_find
    ; (control does not return here linearly)

do_interp_after_find:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save continuation IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; Stack: either (c-addr 0) not found, or (cfa flag) found
    ; Also have original c-addr underneath: (orig_c-addr result flag) or (orig_c-addr cfa flag)
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found

    ; === Found: flag in r0 (1=immediate, -1=normal), cfa on stack ===
    ; DS: [cfa, orig_c-addr]
    ; Drop orig_c-addr: swap, drop
    pop r2               ; r2 = cfa
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [cfa]

    ; Save flag (r0 was popped as flag earlier, but we consumed it in ceq)
    ; Hmm, r0 had the flag but we tested it. We need to re-derive.
    ; Actually after pop r0 (flag) and ceq r0,z, r0 still = flag value
    ; Let's check STATE
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save flag. RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)        ; r0 = STATE
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec      ; STATE=0 → interpreting → execute

    ; Compiling: check if IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2           ; C = (flag == 1 = IMMEDIATE)
    brt interp_exec_compile ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word in compile mode: COMMA the CFA
    ; DS: [cfa], compile it
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save r2 (junk, but need for later)
    pop r0               ; cfa
    ; Store at HERE
    push r0              ; re-push for comma logic
    la r2, interp_comma_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_thread:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_continue

interp_exec_compile:
    ; Execute an IMMEDIATE word while compiling
    ; fall through to interp_exec

interp_exec:
    ; Execute word: cfa on stack
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag (if still there from compiling path)
                        ; For interpret path, this pops cont_IP... that's wrong
    ; Let me rethink the RS management here

    ; RS state depends on path:
    ; From interpret (STATE=0): RS = [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ;   - we need to pop flag, then execute, then return via cont_IP/orig_IP
    ; From IMMEDIATE in compile: RS = [cont_IP, orig_IP] (flag already popped)

    ; Simplify: in both cases, clean RS to [cont_IP, orig_IP], execute cfa
    ; For STATE=0 path, flag is at RS top: pop it
    ; Actually let me restructure. The STATE=0 path jumps here with
    ; RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]. Pop flag:
    ; But the IMMEDIATE path already popped flag and jumps here too.
    ; I need separate labels.

    ; Let me just use a unified approach:
    ; interp_exec is ONLY reached from STATE=0 path with RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag (discard)
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

interp_do_exec:
    ; DS: [cfa], RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; After EXECUTE, we need to continue the interpret loop
    ; Set IP to cont thread that loops back
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute it — when it does NEXT, IP = interp_loop_thread

interp_loop_thread:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; will pop cont_IP, then orig_IP restores caller

interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr_from_find, orig_c-addr]
    ; c-addr_from_find = orig_c-addr (FIND returns it when not found)
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr from FIND
    ; DS: [orig_c-addr]

    ; Try NUMBER
    ; NUMBER expects c-addr on stack
    pop r0               ; orig_c-addr
    push r0              ; keep for error message
    push r0              ; for NUMBER
    la r2, interp_number_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_thread:
    .word do_number
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; DS: [flag, n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag (0=ok)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_got_number

    ; NUMBER failed: print "? " error
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    ; Print "? "
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err_tx1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err_tx2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE to 0
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue interpret loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_got_number:
    ; DS: [n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r2               ; r2 = n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_interp

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    ; Push n, then compile LIT then n via comma
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    ; First push do_lit address, comma it, then push n, comma it
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n again
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile LIT address
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Retrieve saved n from RS
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n was saved 1 slot below current
    ; Hmm, RS is getting complicated. Let me simplify.
    ; After do_comma returns via NEXT to do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]  — n was saved before entering compile_lit_thread
    ; Wait, I saved n but then set r2 to the thread and did NEXT.
    ; The NEXT from do_comma will go to do_interp_compile_n.
    ; When do_interp_compile_n runs, we sw r2,0(r1) saving that IP.
    ; RS: [this_IP, n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; n is at offset 3
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n
    push r0              ; push n for comma
    la r2, interp_comma_cont_thread
    add r1, 3           ; pop this_IP. RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop num_IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_cont_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile n
    .word do_interp_continue_loop

do_interp_continue_loop:
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; Continue interpret loop
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_interp:
    ; Interpreting: number already on stack, continue loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_continue:
    ; After compiling a word, continue the loop
    ; RS: [junk_r2, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop junk
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ------------------------------------------------------------
; QUIT ( -- ) : Outer interpreter loop
; Resets return stack, loops: INTERPRET, print " ok\n" if STATE=0
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_quit:
    .word entry_interpret
    .byte 4
    .byte 81, 85, 73, 84
do_quit:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP to 0x0F0000

quit_loop:
    ; Set IP to quit_thread, run NEXT
    la r2, quit_inner_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

quit_inner_thread:
    .word do_interpret
    .word do_quit_prompt
    .word do_quit_loop_back

do_quit_prompt:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Check STATE: if 0, print " ok\n"
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brf quit_prompt_done  ; compiling, no prompt

    ; Print " ok\n"
    la r2, -65280
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
quit_p1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 111          ; 'o'
    push r0
quit_p2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z

content.text:
This is very messy. The interpret loop has deeply nested RS management with multiple thread jumps that are very hard to reason about. Let me take a fundamentally different approach — rewrite the entire INTERPRET + QUIT section cleanly. The key insight: instead of using mini-threads and NEXT-driven callbacks, make INTERPRET a single monolithic primitive that directly calls WORD, FIND, and NUMBER by jumping to them with carefully managed RS.

Actually, an even better approach: use a colon-definition style but with a simpler control flow. Let me scrap the current INTERPRET/QUIT code and rewrite it from scratch with a much simpler structure.

input.old_string:
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; INTERPRET ( -- ) : Interpret one line of input
; Reads tokens with WORD, dispatches via FIND/NUMBER
; Uses a threaded-code approach for cleaner flow
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84
do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r2, interpret_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; INTERPRET is implemented as a colon word via a thread
interpret_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; returns to caller

; The main dispatch logic — must be a primitive since it branches
do_interpret_dispatch:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [IP]

    ; Check if word is empty (length = 0)
    pop r0               ; c-addr from WORD
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_word
    ; Empty word = end of input, just return
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_word:
    ; Save c-addr for potential NUMBER fallback
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]

    ; Try FIND
    ; FIND expects c-addr on stack, returns (c-addr 0) or (cfa flag)
    ; We need to call FIND as a primitive
    push r0              ; duplicate c-addr for FIND. DS: [c-addr, c-addr]

    ; Save IP on RS, call do_find via mini-thread
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find
    .word do_interp_after_find
    ; (control does not return here linearly)

do_interp_after_find:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save continuation IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; Stack: either (c-addr 0) not found, or (cfa flag) found
    ; Also have original c-addr underneath: (orig_c-addr result flag) or (orig_c-addr cfa flag)
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found

    ; === Found: flag in r0 (1=immediate, -1=normal), cfa on stack ===
    ; DS: [cfa, orig_c-addr]
    ; Drop orig_c-addr: swap, drop
    pop r2               ; r2 = cfa
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [cfa]

    ; Save flag (r0 was popped as flag earlier, but we consumed it in ceq)
    ; Hmm, r0 had the flag but we tested it. We need to re-derive.
    ; Actually after pop r0 (flag) and ceq r0,z, r0 still = flag value
    ; Let's check STATE
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save flag. RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)        ; r0 = STATE
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec      ; STATE=0 → interpreting → execute

    ; Compiling: check if IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2           ; C = (flag == 1 = IMMEDIATE)
    brt interp_exec_compile ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word in compile mode: COMMA the CFA
    ; DS: [cfa], compile it
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save r2 (junk, but need for later)
    pop r0               ; cfa
    ; Store at HERE
    push r0              ; re-push for comma logic
    la r2, interp_comma_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_thread:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_continue

interp_exec_compile:
    ; Execute an IMMEDIATE word while compiling
    ; fall through to interp_exec

interp_exec:
    ; Execute word: cfa on stack
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag (if still there from compiling path)
                        ; For interpret path, this pops cont_IP... that's wrong
    ; Let me rethink the RS management here

    ; RS state depends on path:
    ; From interpret (STATE=0): RS = [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ;   - we need to pop flag, then execute, then return via cont_IP/orig_IP
    ; From IMMEDIATE in compile: RS = [cont_IP, orig_IP] (flag already popped)

    ; Simplify: in both cases, clean RS to [cont_IP, orig_IP], execute cfa
    ; For STATE=0 path, flag is at RS top: pop it
    ; Actually let me restructure. The STATE=0 path jumps here with
    ; RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]. Pop flag:
    ; But the IMMEDIATE path already popped flag and jumps here too.
    ; I need separate labels.

    ; Let me just use a unified approach:
    ; interp_exec is ONLY reached from STATE=0 path with RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag (discard)
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

interp_do_exec:
    ; DS: [cfa], RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; After EXECUTE, we need to continue the interpret loop
    ; Set IP to cont thread that loops back
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute it — when it does NEXT, IP = interp_loop_thread

interp_loop_thread:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; will pop cont_IP, then orig_IP restores caller

interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr_from_find, orig_c-addr]
    ; c-addr_from_find = orig_c-addr (FIND returns it when not found)
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr from FIND
    ; DS: [orig_c-addr]

    ; Try NUMBER
    ; NUMBER expects c-addr on stack
    pop r0               ; orig_c-addr
    push r0              ; keep for error message
    push r0              ; for NUMBER
    la r2, interp_number_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_thread:
    .word do_number
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; DS: [flag, n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag (0=ok)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_got_number

    ; NUMBER failed: print "? " error
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    ; Print "? "
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err_tx1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err_tx2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE to 0
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue interpret loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_got_number:
    ; DS: [n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r2               ; r2 = n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_interp

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    ; Push n, then compile LIT then n via comma
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    ; First push do_lit address, comma it, then push n, comma it
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n again
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile LIT address
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Retrieve saved n from RS
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n was saved 1 slot below current
    ; Hmm, RS is getting complicated. Let me simplify.
    ; After do_comma returns via NEXT to do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]  — n was saved before entering compile_lit_thread
    ; Wait, I saved n but then set r2 to the thread and did NEXT.
    ; The NEXT from do_comma will go to do_interp_compile_n.
    ; When do_interp_compile_n runs, we sw r2,0(r1) saving that IP.
    ; RS: [this_IP, n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; n is at offset 3
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n
    push r0              ; push n for comma
    la r2, interp_comma_cont_thread
    add r1, 3           ; pop this_IP. RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop num_IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_cont_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile n
    .word do_interp_continue_loop

do_interp_continue_loop:
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; Continue interpret loop
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_interp:
    ; Interpreting: number already on stack, continue loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_continue:
    ; After compiling a word, continue the loop
    ; RS: [junk_r2, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop junk
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ------------------------------------------------------------
; QUIT ( -- ) : Outer interpreter loop
; Resets return stack, loops: INTERPRET, print " ok\n" if STATE=0
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_quit:
    .word entry_interpret
    .byte 4
    .byte 81, 85, 73, 84
do_quit:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP to 0x0F0000

quit_loop:
    ; Set IP to quit_thread, run NEXT
    la r2, quit_inner_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

quit_inner_thread:
    .word do_interpret
    .word do_quit_prompt
    .word do_quit_loop_back

do_quit_prompt:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Check STATE: if 0, print " ok\n"
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brf quit_prompt_done  ; compiling, no prompt

    ; Print " ok\n"
    la r2, -65280
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
quit_p1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 111          ; 'o'
    push r0
quit_p2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 107          ; 'k'
    push r0
quit_p3:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p3
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 10           ; '\n'
    push r0
quit_p4:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p4
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

quit_prompt_done:
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_quit_loop_back:
    ; Loop back to quit — reset RS and restart
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP
    la r2, quit_inner_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3

input.new_string:
; ============================================================
; INTERPRET — single monolithic primitive
; ============================================================
; Strategy: INTERPRET is a primitive loop. It directly jumps to
; do_word, do_find, do_number by setting up mini-threads.
; The key invariant: upon entry and at each loop iteration,
; RS has exactly [caller_IP] at its base.
;
; Flow: WORD → check empty → FIND → dispatch found/not-found
;       → NUMBER → dispatch number/error → loop
; ============================================================
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84

do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; RS: [caller_IP]
    ; Start the token loop by calling WORD
    la r2, interp_word_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_word_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interp_check_empty

; === Check if token is empty (end of line) ===
do_interp_check_empty:
    ; IP points past this entry in interp_word_thread
    ; but we won't use it — we manage our own flow
    pop r0               ; c-addr
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_token
    ; Empty → return to caller
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; caller_IP
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_token:
    ; c-addr in r0, push for FIND
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find        ; ( c-addr -- c-addr 0 | cfa flag )
    .word do_interp_after_find

; === After FIND ===
do_interp_after_find:
    pop r0               ; flag (0=not found, 1=IMMEDIATE, -1=normal)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found
    ; Found! r0 = flag, DS: [cfa]
    ; Save flag on RS
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; RS: [flag, caller_IP]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec_word ; STATE=0 → execute

    ; Compiling: check IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt interp_exec_word ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word + compile mode → COMMA the CFA
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag. RS: [caller_IP]
    ; DS: [cfa] — COMMA will consume it
    la r2, interp_comma_continue
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_continue:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_next_token

interp_exec_word:
    ; Execute the word. DS: [cfa], RS: [flag, caller_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag. RS: [caller_IP]
    ; Set IP so after the executed word does NEXT, we continue the loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute — NEXT will use r2 (interp_after_exec)

interp_after_exec:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interp_check_empty

; === Not found — try NUMBER ===
interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr], RS: [caller_IP]
    ; FIND returned c-addr when not found
    ; Duplicate for error reporting
    pop r0
    push r0
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr, c-addr]
    la r2, interp_num_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_thread:
    .word do_number      ; ( c-addr -- n flag )
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    ; DS: [flag, n, c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_number_ok

    ; NUMBER failed — print "? " and continue
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_ok:
    ; DS: [n, c-addr]
    pop r2               ; n
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_leave ; interpreting → leave on stack

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS. RS: [n, caller_IP]
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0              ; push do_lit address for COMMA
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile do_lit
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, caller_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [caller_IP]
    push r0              ; push n for COMMA
    la r2, interp_comma_continue
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_next_token:
    ; Continue the interpret loop — get next token
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_leave:
    ; n on stack, continue loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; QUIT — outer interpreter loop
; ============================================================
entry_quit:
    .word entry_interpret
    .byte 4
    .byte 81, 85, 73, 84
do_quit:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP to 0x0F0000
    la r2, quit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

quit_thread:
    .word do_interpret
    .word do_quit_prompt
    .word do_quit_restart

do_quit_prompt:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brf quit_no_prompt

    ; Print " ok\n"
    la r2, -65280
    lc r0, 32
    push r0
quit_p1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 111
    push r0
quit_p2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 107
    push r0
quit_p3:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p3
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 10
    push r0
quit_p4:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p4
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

quit_no_prompt:
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_quit_restart:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP
    la r2, quit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

toolUseResult.oldString:
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; INTERPRET ( -- ) : Interpret one line of input
; Reads tokens with WORD, dispatches via FIND/NUMBER
; Uses a threaded-code approach for cleaner flow
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84
do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r2, interpret_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; INTERPRET is implemented as a colon word via a thread
interpret_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; returns to caller

; The main dispatch logic — must be a primitive since it branches
do_interpret_dispatch:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [IP]

    ; Check if word is empty (length = 0)
    pop r0               ; c-addr from WORD
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_word
    ; Empty word = end of input, just return
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_word:
    ; Save c-addr for potential NUMBER fallback
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]

    ; Try FIND
    ; FIND expects c-addr on stack, returns (c-addr 0) or (cfa flag)
    ; We need to call FIND as a primitive
    push r0              ; duplicate c-addr for FIND. DS: [c-addr, c-addr]

    ; Save IP on RS, call do_find via mini-thread
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find
    .word do_interp_after_find
    ; (control does not return here linearly)

do_interp_after_find:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save continuation IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; Stack: either (c-addr 0) not found, or (cfa flag) found
    ; Also have original c-addr underneath: (orig_c-addr result flag) or (orig_c-addr cfa flag)
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found

    ; === Found: flag in r0 (1=immediate, -1=normal), cfa on stack ===
    ; DS: [cfa, orig_c-addr]
    ; Drop orig_c-addr: swap, drop
    pop r2               ; r2 = cfa
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [cfa]

    ; Save flag (r0 was popped as flag earlier, but we consumed it in ceq)
    ; Hmm, r0 had the flag but we tested it. We need to re-derive.
    ; Actually after pop r0 (flag) and ceq r0,z, r0 still = flag value
    ; Let's check STATE
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save flag. RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)        ; r0 = STATE
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec      ; STATE=0 → interpreting → execute

    ; Compiling: check if IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2           ; C = (flag == 1 = IMMEDIATE)
    brt interp_exec_compile ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word in compile mode: COMMA the CFA
    ; DS: [cfa], compile it
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save r2 (junk, but need for later)
    pop r0               ; cfa
    ; Store at HERE
    push r0              ; re-push for comma logic
    la r2, interp_comma_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_thread:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_continue

interp_exec_compile:
    ; Execute an IMMEDIATE word while compiling
    ; fall through to interp_exec

interp_exec:
    ; Execute word: cfa on stack
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag (if still there from compiling path)
                        ; For interpret path, this pops cont_IP... that's wrong
    ; Let me rethink the RS management here

    ; RS state depends on path:
    ; From interpret (STATE=0): RS = [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ;   - we need to pop flag, then execute, then return via cont_IP/orig_IP
    ; From IMMEDIATE in compile: RS = [cont_IP, orig_IP] (flag already popped)

    ; Simplify: in both cases, clean RS to [cont_IP, orig_IP], execute cfa
    ; For STATE=0 path, flag is at RS top: pop it
    ; Actually let me restructure. The STATE=0 path jumps here with
    ; RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]. Pop flag:
    ; But the IMMEDIATE path already popped flag and jumps here too.
    ; I need separate labels.

    ; Let me just use a unified approach:
    ; interp_exec is ONLY reached from STATE=0 path with RS: [flag, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag (discard)
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]

interp_do_exec:
    ; DS: [cfa], RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; After EXECUTE, we need to continue the interpret loop
    ; Set IP to cont thread that loops back
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute it — when it does NEXT, IP = interp_loop_thread

interp_loop_thread:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interpret_dispatch
    .word do_exit        ; will pop cont_IP, then orig_IP restores caller

interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr_from_find, orig_c-addr]
    ; c-addr_from_find = orig_c-addr (FIND returns it when not found)
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr from FIND
    ; DS: [orig_c-addr]

    ; Try NUMBER
    ; NUMBER expects c-addr on stack
    pop r0               ; orig_c-addr
    push r0              ; keep for error message
    push r0              ; for NUMBER
    la r2, interp_number_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_thread:
    .word do_number
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]

    ; DS: [flag, n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag (0=ok)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_got_number

    ; NUMBER failed: print "? " error
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    ; Print "? "
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err_tx1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err_tx2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err_tx2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE to 0
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue interpret loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_got_number:
    ; DS: [n, orig_c-addr]
    pop r2               ; r2 = n
    pop r0               ; discard orig_c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_interp

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    ; Push n, then compile LIT then n via comma
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    ; First push do_lit address, comma it, then push n, comma it
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n again
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile LIT address
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Retrieve saved n from RS
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n was saved 1 slot below current
    ; Hmm, RS is getting complicated. Let me simplify.
    ; After do_comma returns via NEXT to do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]  — n was saved before entering compile_lit_thread
    ; Wait, I saved n but then set r2 to the thread and did NEXT.
    ; The NEXT from do_comma will go to do_interp_compile_n.
    ; When do_interp_compile_n runs, we sw r2,0(r1) saving that IP.
    ; RS: [this_IP, n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; n is at offset 3
    lw r0, 3(r1)        ; n
    push r0              ; push n for comma
    la r2, interp_comma_cont_thread
    add r1, 3           ; pop this_IP. RS: [n, num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [num_IP, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop num_IP. RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_cont_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile n
    .word do_interp_continue_loop

do_interp_continue_loop:
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    ; Continue interpret loop
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_interp:
    ; Interpreting: number already on stack, continue loop
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; num_IP
    add r1, 3           ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_continue:
    ; After compiling a word, continue the loop
    ; RS: [junk_r2, cont_IP, orig_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop junk
    ; RS: [cont_IP, orig_IP]
    la r2, interp_loop_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ------------------------------------------------------------
; QUIT ( -- ) : Outer interpreter loop
; Resets return stack, loops: INTERPRET, print " ok\n" if STATE=0
; ------------------------------------------------------------
entry_quit:
    .word entry_interpret
    .byte 4
    .byte 81, 85, 73, 84
do_quit:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP to 0x0F0000

quit_loop:
    ; Set IP to quit_thread, run NEXT
    la r2, quit_inner_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

quit_inner_thread:
    .word do_interpret
    .word do_quit_prompt
    .word do_quit_loop_back

do_quit_prompt:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    ; Check STATE: if 0, print " ok\n"
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brf quit_prompt_done  ; compiling, no prompt

    ; Print " ok\n"
    la r2, -65280
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
quit_p1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 111          ; 'o'
    push r0
quit_p2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 107          ; 'k'
    push r0
quit_p3:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p3
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 10           ; '\n'
    push r0
quit_p4:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p4
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

quit_prompt_done:
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_quit_loop_back:
    ; Loop back to quit — reset RS and restart
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP
    la r2, quit_inner_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)
toolUseResult.newString:
; ============================================================
; INTERPRET — single monolithic primitive
; ============================================================
; Strategy: INTERPRET is a primitive loop. It directly jumps to
; do_word, do_find, do_number by setting up mini-threads.
; The key invariant: upon entry and at each loop iteration,
; RS has exactly [caller_IP] at its base.
;
; Flow: WORD → check empty → FIND → dispatch found/not-found
;       → NUMBER → dispatch number/error → loop
; ============================================================
entry_interpret:
    .word entry_dot
    .byte 9
    .byte 73, 78, 84, 69, 82, 80, 82, 69, 84

do_interpret:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; RS: [caller_IP]
    ; Start the token loop by calling WORD
    la r2, interp_word_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_word_thread:
    .word do_word        ; ( -- c-addr )
    .word do_interp_check_empty

; === Check if token is empty (end of line) ===
do_interp_check_empty:
    ; IP points past this entry in interp_word_thread
    ; but we won't use it — we manage our own flow
    pop r0               ; c-addr
    lbu r2, 0(r0)       ; length
    ceq r2, z
    brf interp_have_token
    ; Empty → return to caller
    lw r2, 0(r1)        ; caller_IP
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_have_token:
    ; c-addr in r0, push for FIND
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr]
    la r2, interp_find_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_find_thread:
    .word do_find        ; ( c-addr -- c-addr 0 | cfa flag )
    .word do_interp_after_find

; === After FIND ===
do_interp_after_find:
    pop r0               ; flag (0=not found, 1=IMMEDIATE, -1=normal)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_not_found
    ; Found! r0 = flag, DS: [cfa]
    ; Save flag on RS
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; RS: [flag, caller_IP]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_exec_word ; STATE=0 → execute

    ; Compiling: check IMMEDIATE
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; flag
    lcu r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt interp_exec_word ; IMMEDIATE → execute even in compile mode

    ; Normal word + compile mode → COMMA the CFA
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag. RS: [caller_IP]
    ; DS: [cfa] — COMMA will consume it
    la r2, interp_comma_continue
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_comma_continue:
    .word do_comma
    .word do_interp_next_token

interp_exec_word:
    ; Execute the word. DS: [cfa], RS: [flag, caller_IP]
    add r1, 3           ; pop flag. RS: [caller_IP]
    ; Set IP so after the executed word does NEXT, we continue the loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    pop r0               ; cfa
    jmp (r0)             ; execute — NEXT will use r2 (interp_after_exec)

interp_after_exec:
    .word do_word
    .word do_interp_check_empty

; === Not found — try NUMBER ===
interp_not_found:
    ; DS: [c-addr], RS: [caller_IP]
    ; FIND returned c-addr when not found
    ; Duplicate for error reporting
    pop r0
    push r0
    push r0              ; DS: [c-addr, c-addr]
    la r2, interp_num_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_thread:
    .word do_number      ; ( c-addr -- n flag )
    .word do_interp_after_number

do_interp_after_number:
    ; DS: [flag, n, c-addr]
    pop r0               ; flag
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_number_ok

    ; NUMBER failed — print "? " and continue
    pop r0               ; discard n
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr
    lc r0, 63           ; '?'
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
interp_err1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 32           ; ' '
    push r0
interp_err2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt interp_err2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Reset STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lc r2, 0
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    ; Continue loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_number_ok:
    ; DS: [n, c-addr]
    pop r2               ; n
    pop r0               ; discard c-addr
    push r2              ; DS: [n]

    ; Check STATE
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brt interp_num_leave ; interpreting → leave on stack

    ; Compiling: compile LIT, n
    pop r0               ; n
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)        ; save n on RS. RS: [n, caller_IP]
    la r0, do_lit
    push r0              ; push do_lit address for COMMA
    la r2, interp_compile_lit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_compile_lit_thread:
    .word do_comma       ; compile do_lit
    .word do_interp_compile_n

do_interp_compile_n:
    ; RS: [n, caller_IP]
    lw r0, 0(r1)        ; n
    add r1, 3           ; pop n. RS: [caller_IP]
    push r0              ; push n for COMMA
    la r2, interp_comma_continue
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_interp_next_token:
    ; Continue the interpret loop — get next token
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

interp_num_leave:
    ; n on stack, continue loop
    la r2, interp_after_exec
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; QUIT — outer interpreter loop
; ============================================================
entry_quit:
    .word entry_interpret
    .byte 4
    .byte 81, 85, 73, 84
do_quit:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP to 0x0F0000
    la r2, quit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

quit_thread:
    .word do_interpret
    .word do_quit_prompt
    .word do_quit_restart

do_quit_prompt:
    add r1, -3
    sw r2, 0(r1)        ; save IP
    la r0, var_state_val
    lw r0, 0(r0)
    ceq r0, z
    brf quit_no_prompt

    ; Print " ok\n"
    la r2, -65280
    lc r0, 32
    push r0
quit_p1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 111
    push r0
quit_p2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 107
    push r0
quit_p3:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p3
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 10
    push r0
quit_p4:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt quit_p4
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

quit_no_prompt:
    lw r2, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

do_quit_restart:
    la r1, 983040        ; reset RSP
    la r2, quit_thread
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)
